latestCycle.goal: reduce wrong-signed Andes lee suppression by damping lee-side terrain subsidence without removing upslope lift
latestCycle.verifiedImprovement: default lee-side subsidence damping is now 0.35, improving Andes precipRatio from 0.8353 to 1.0099 at 75600 and from 0.8217 to 0.9226 at 105480 while Rockies stayed windward-favored and the full validation suite passed

blockingGaps: broader Earth-like realism still needs fresh mature-window re-audits in the live app with lee-side terrain-subsidence damping active across circulation, clouds, storms, and precipitation placement
orographic realism improved but is not solved: the Andes mature-window precipRatio now clears 1.0 at 75600 but is still 0.9226 at 105480, and the terrain-flow sample geometry still needs refinement
runtime smoothness is still not signed off; the latest live run now has healthy telemetry and still reports earth_update_p95_high, earth_update_max_high, and wind_targets_failing
world-class status still requires realism and smoothness to pass in the same fresh live run
cycleInputs: latest runtime summary from npm run agent:summarize-runtime-log

browserObservation.note: reused canonical localhost solo-mode tab emitted 252 live weather-log lines after the restart, so runtime logging is healthy enough again to guide future smoothness work

defaultNextPriority: stay on orographic realism and re-audit the Andes with lee-side subsidence damping active, prioritizing the remaining later-window shortfall and terrain-flow sample-geometry mismatch
target the next physics cycle at one concrete src-side fix area before taking any new diagnostics-only detour
use browser verification only after an offline candidate looks promising, and keep it to one canonical localhost run
when smoothness becomes the blocker again, use the fresh non-empty runtime telemetry before changing renderer or worker performance behavior
keep validation on the clean world-class checkout only
